Career Center

Career Center’s Goals

MENU

Our

Career Center’s

purpose is leading our 
students to know themselves during their studies, supporting them on 
their career planning, giving information about business life’s 
expectations and guiding them to jobs that they can improve themselves 
after their graduation.

With our Center’s collaboration with the business world, our students
 have possibility to work as part-time or do internship with our 
collaborators subject to their studies.

Career Center’s Practices:

Our Center enables students to work in the positions of our collaborators or do internship as part of the

depending on the companies’ needs. Thus, our business life experienced 
students start to earn money while they are still studying. In this 
process, our institute pays the insurance premium for possible 
occupational diseases and occupational accidents. Most of the part time 
workers and long-term interns among our students continue to work as 
full time after their graduations.

As part of the ‘’Business Life Program’’, first ever applied in our 
university in Turkey, we aim to make our students to know themselves 
before their graduation, getting sense of self awareness, making 
decisions about their career choices and gaining the information about 
business life to be successful. We do these to our preparatory year 
students by

‘’Business and Life Courses’’

and to our junior and senior students by

‘’Preparation Courses for Graduation’’

.
 At the same time, we support our young students to fit in to this new 
environment which has its own dynamics, as fast as possible since their 
very first step to business life.

Courses’ Index:

Increases our students’ self awareness, enables to determine their strong sides and know themselves better,

Enables them to specify their vocation and competence about careers,

Enables them to determine their career options,

Enables them to create personal vision and aim concept,

Introduces them to  basic notions of business life such as motivation, performance, pro activity,

Enables them to manage the changing dynamics of becoming a ‘’worker’’ from a ‘’student’’,

Enables them to create their own personal image and gain communicational skills,

Enables them to understand searching job techniques and use them creatively and efficiently,

Enables them to write an effective CV and prepare them to interviews.

Career Center’s Role on Graduates:

Connections with our graduates and monitoring graduates

is
 another important task of our center. We have been following our 
graduates since 2007. As much as our students, we also keep in touch 
with our graduates via social media, e-mails, telephone, student clubs 
and/or face to face meetings.

With the workshops focused on industries’ expectations, our 
students get to meet with business life early; and once they are 
graduated, with our courses, role model workshops and personal 
consultancy services, they are supported to find jobs depending on their
 qualifications.

Along with our ‘’The University Closest to Business Life’’ motto, 
we have agreements both on public (such as İŞKUR) and private company 
levels. Therefore, we play missionary role for our students’ 
decarceration to industry.

To let our successful graduates to mentor our young students,

OKAN

ALUMNI ASSOCIATION

has been established. Our university dedicated itself to communicate with our graduates by any means.

FACULTIES AND CAREER CENTER WORKING TOGETHER: WORKPLACE SUPPORTED EDUCATION PROGRAM ( COOPerative Learning ):

Workplace Supported Education ( COOPerative Learning ) program is predicated on 
student’s practical working in a business during his/her undergraduate 
education.

COOPerative Learning’s goals;

Familiarizing students to workplaces related to their undergraduate programs,

Enabling students to reinforce their information and manners 
related to education and experience that they have learnt during their 
undergraduate program by practicing,

By up skilling students to be able to let them use their 
theoretical information and use them practically,  COOPerative Learning  makes adaptation
 time to business life after graduation shorter,

As the  COOPerative Learning  program’s education due, enabling students to learn 
their responsibilities, relations, organization and producing process, 
getting to know new technologies at their workplace; gaining good 
communication habits with colleagues and customers.

COOPERATIVE LEARNING'S DURATION and ACHIEVEMENT CONDITIONS:

For Faculty of Engineering;

Mission of the program is letting the students get involved in the 
workplaces related to their studies for one semester for every year (8 
weeks/semester) since their second year; and improve themselves with the
 practical information alongside with the theoretical information they 
learnt in the university. As the end of the first semester being the 
earliest start and the end of the eighth semester is the latest end, 40 
working days in a semester, the program is finished in total of 120 
working days in 3 semesters.

Students are able to participate in this program by their will. 
Students state their demand to participate in this program with petition
 written to  COOPerative Learning  Faculty/School Coordinator; and gets accepted to 
program by board of management decision. The students, who is accepted 
to this program, are also supposed to register to program in the 
electronic system.

To be able to get the department’s  COOPerative Learning  certificate, students have
 to receive  COOPerative Learning  training and complete it successfully by working three
 (3) semesters, eight (8) weeks in the workplaces (total of 24 weeks or 
120 days). Getting a training at workplace non-stop is essential. To 
complete the total amount of time, continuousness in the specified days 
of the week is a must.

Students have their common trainings during summer and/or academic period.

For Faculty of Economics and Administrative Sciences, and School of Applied Sciences Gastronomy Department;

At the second half of the senior year (eighth semester), students are
 enable to get involved with the activities in the workplaces related to
 their studies and able to gain practical information alongside with 
their theoretical information learnt at university.

Students are able to participate in this program by their will. 
Students state their demand to participate in this program with petition
 written to  COOPerative Learning  Faculty/School Coordinator; and gets accepted to 
program by board of management decision. The students, who is accepted 
to this program, are also supposed to register to program in the 
electronic system.

To be able to get the department’s  COOPerative Learning  certificate, students have
 to receive   COOPerative Learning  training for 14 weeks and complete it successfully.

Students have their common trainings during academic period.
